The '''Oni''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Uru]]. They were a militant species who were often known to work off-planet as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. [[Gender/Legends|Female]] Oni were electrophoretic and could store static electricity in a unique [[Capacitor/Legends|capacitor]] gland within their bodies. This species' members included a female [[mercenary]] who was a patient of [[Lieutenant]] [[Kornell Divini]]'s on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]].<ref name="MS2">''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
According to [[Joseph Bongiorno]], this species was intended by [[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]] authors [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]] as a reference to the Oni species of Oniboshi in the Japanese anime and manga series [[Wikipedia:Urusei_Yatsura|''Urusei Yatsura'']] by [[Wikipedia:Rumiko_Takahashi|Rumiko Takahashi]]. As both species are militant, both species' females are shown to be capable of generating electricity, and the [[Star Wars]] Oni come from Uru as in Urusei, this would indeed seem to be likely.<ref name="timelines" >{{WebCite|url=https://www.starwarstimeline.net/Supernatural_Encounters.htm|text=I heard there are a lot of crossovers. What is that about?|work=www.starwarstimeline.net|quote=in MedStar II: Jedi Healer, Michael Reaves and Steve Perry reference the Oni of Uru, which come from the 1980s anime-manga series, Urusei Yatsura, which itself referenced Star Wars.|archivedate=20250211153149}}</ref>
